title: cephaelis leucantha
intro: Cephaelis leucantha, also known as White Foxglove or White Hellebore, is a flowering plant species native to Central and South America, characterized by its white, bell-shaped flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as an emetic and purgative, and its potential to treat conditions such as fever and rheumatism. The health benefits of Cephaelis leucantha are attributed to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various diseases, including arthritis and respiratory issues. Cephaelis leucantha is typically prepared as an herbal tea or tincture, and its extracts are also used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
